intended_use: Autoplaque is intended to provide an optimized non-invasive application to analyze coronary anatomy and pathology and aid in determining treatment paths from a set of Computed Tomography (CT) Angiogra
intended_use_full: Autoplaque is intended to provide an optimized non-invasive application to analyze coronary anatomy and pathology and aid in determining treatment paths from a set of Computed Tomography (CT) Angiographic images. Autoplaque is a workstation-based post processing application. It is a non-invasive diagnostic reading software intended for use by cardiologists and radiologists as an interactive tool for viewing and analyzing cardiac CT data for determining the presence and extent of coronary plaques and luminal stenoses.
target_condition: Coronary artery disease; presence and extent of coronary plaques and luminal stenoses
ground_truth: Board-certified imaging cardiologist (US study); two cardiologists with one additional highly experienced radiologist to resolve discrepancies (non-US study)
other_metrics: Intraclass correlation coefficient and correlation coefficient reported for total plaque volume, calcified plaque volume, non-calcified plaque volume, and diameter stenosis described as 'excellent agreement' in both US and non-US populations; analysis time 5.7 seconds per patient versus 25-30 minutes for experts
